trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: This creature's attacks are calibrated to Challenge 14 (tier 3), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. Its listed CR reflects the danger of its attacks, not its durability: a single solid hit or area spell from a 14th-level character usually drops it. For encounter budgeting, treat it as far less durable than a true CR 14 monster (see the Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer for roster math).

trait.name: Paradox-Touched
entries: The goblin was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its hide is tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). It has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}—it has already seen the end of the world.

trait.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The goblin takes the {@action Disengage} or {@action Hide} action as a Bonus Action on each of its turns.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The goblin makes two Paradox Scimitar attacks.

action.name: Paradox Scimitar
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 11}, reach 5 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Necrotic damage.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the goblin is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Necrotic damage from the imploding seam.

A frontline cutter of the Recopied Warband—a violet shadow of a goblin that swings a blade edged in unmade dark. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} for the full encounter.

trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: This creature's attacks are calibrated to Challenge 14 (tier 3), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. Its listed CR reflects the danger of its attacks, not its durability: a single solid hit or area spell from a 14th-level character usually drops it. For encounter budgeting, treat it as far less durable than a true CR 14 monster (see the Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer for roster math).

trait.name: Paradox-Touched
entries: The goblin was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its hide is tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). It has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}—it has already seen the end of the world.

trait.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The goblin takes the {@action Disengage} or {@action Hide} action as a Bonus Action on each of its turns.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The goblin makes two Paradox Shortbow attacks.

action.name: Paradox Shortbow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 11}, range 80/320 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) Piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Force damage.

action.name: Starfall Volley {@recharge 5}
entries: The goblin looses an arrow that bursts into falling starlight at a point it can see within 150 feet. {@actSave dex} {@dc 18}, each creature in a 15-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on that point. {@actSaveFail} 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Force damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the goblin is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Force damage from the imploding seam.

A recopied skirmisher that fires arrows of bottled starlight from the edges of the fight. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} for the full encounter.

trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: This creature's attacks and spells are calibrated to Challenge 14 (tier 3), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. Its listed CR reflects the danger of its magic, not its durability: a single solid hit or area spell from a 14th-level character usually drops it.

trait.name: Paradox-Touched
entries: The goblin was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its hide is tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). It has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}—it has already seen the end of the world.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The goblin casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 19}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +11 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The goblin casts a spell or makes two Hex Bolt attacks.

action.name: Hex Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 11}, range 120 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) Necrotic damage, and the target can't regain Hit Points until the end of the goblin's next turn.

reaction.name: Curse of the Unmade
entries: When a creature the goblin can see within 60 feet reduces it to 0 Hit Points, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 19} Wisdom saving throw or take 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Psychic damage and have Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the end of its next turn, as a stranger's memories flood in.

entries.name: The Recopied Warband
entries: This warband of goblins and kobolds wandered too long inside the violet ring around Vorthrax's sleeping-hollow, and the paradox recopied them. Each is the same creature it always was—just repainted for the end of the world: hide gone violet, eyes lit with stray starlight, every blow edged with a sliver of the unmade dark. They are louder, faster, and far deadlier than any goblin or kobold has a right to be... and just as fragile as they ever were. The hexer leads, because it remembers leading—over and over, in lives that no longer happened.

entries.name: Design: Glass-Cannon Minions (CR 14)
entries: Every member of the warband is built as a {@b glass cannon}: its attacks, save DCs, and damage are calibrated to Challenge 14 (tier 3), so a single one is a genuine threat to a 14th-level character—but its Hit Points are minion-grade, so it drops to almost any solid hit or area spell. The CR 14 label is the threat of the attack, not the toughness of the body.

This breaks the normal CR-to-XP math on purpose. By the book, six CR 14 monsters would be an apocalyptic 69,000 XP; in practice these die so fast that the effective difficulty is roughly that of treating each as a {@b CR 4–5} creature. Budget by their fragility, not their label.

The six variants: {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Cutter|ParadoxWarband} and {@creature Paradox-Touched Kobold Clawfoot|ParadoxWarband} (melee warriors), {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Stinger|ParadoxWarband} and {@creature Paradox-Touched Kobold Slinger|ParadoxWarband} (archers), and the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} and {@creature Paradox-Touched Kobold Star-Scale|ParadoxWarband} (casters).

entries.name: Running It for Four 14th-Level Characters
entries: Because they are glass cannons, this fight is {@b swingy}: whoever lands the first big turn shapes it. Suggested rosters:

items: {@b Sharp brawl (recommended):} 1 caster + 1 archer + 2 warriors (4 total)—a tense, fast 2–3 round fight that ends when the party's area damage lands.

{@b The full warband:} both casters + 2 archers + 4 warriors (8 total)—genuinely dangerous in round 1, then collapses quickly. Open with the casters' control and the archers' Volley/Scatter before the party scatters them.
{@b Endless tide:} feed them in waves of 3–4 each round to evoke the region recopying its dead—thematically perfect, and it lets you dial lethality live.

entries: Tactics: casters open with {@spell hunger of hadar|XPHB}, {@spell fear|XPHB}, or {@spell hold monster|XPHB} to pin the party; archers spend {@b Starfall Volley}/{@b Scatter Shot} on clusters; warriors use Nimble Escape and Pack Tactics to gang a single isolated character. Lean into the {@b Unmade Flicker} death bursts—every minion that dies stings whoever killed it, so melee characters take a slow toll wading through the horde.

Flavor note: these are the literal punchline of the campaign's running joke—the level-20 goblin in a different color. When the party recognizes the silhouette of a common goblin and then it hits like a CR 14, that dissonance is the point.

trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: This creature's attacks are calibrated to Challenge 14 (tier 3), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. Its listed CR reflects the danger of its attacks, not its durability: a single solid hit or area spell from a 14th-level character usually drops it. For encounter budgeting, treat it as far less durable than a true CR 14 monster (see the Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer for roster math).

trait.name: Paradox-Touched
entries: The kobold was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its scales are tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). It has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}, and the recopying has burned away its kind's usual Sunlight Sensitivity.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The kobold has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the kobold's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kobold makes two Paradox Claw attacks.

action.name: Paradox Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 11}, reach 5 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Necrotic damage.

bonus.name: Skitter
entries: The kobold takes the {@action Disengage} action.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the kobold is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Necrotic damage from the imploding seam.
A recopied kobold that fights in violet packs, claws trailing unmade dark. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} for the full encounter.

trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: This creature's attacks are calibrated to Challenge 14 (tier 3), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. For encounter budgeting, treat it as far less durable than a true CR 14 monster (see the Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer for roster math).

entries: The kobold was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its scales are tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The kobold has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the kobold's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kobold makes two Paradox Sling attacks.

action.name: Paradox Sling
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 11}, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Force damage.

action.name: Scatter Shot {@recharge 5}
entries: The kobold whirls a spray of starlit shot in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSave dex} {@dc 17}, each creature in that area. {@actSaveFail} 21 ({@damage 6d6}) Force damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the kobold is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Force damage from the imploding seam.

A recopied kobold that pelts the party with bottled starlight from behind its packmates. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} for the full encounter.

entries: This creature's spells are calibrated to Challenge 14 (tier 3), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. entries: The kobold has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the kobold's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The kobold casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 19}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +11 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kobold makes two Star Bolt attacks.

action.name: Star Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 11}, range 120 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) Force damage.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the kobold is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Force damage from the imploding seam.

A recopied scale-sorcerer whose bloodline magic now burns in the colors of the unmade sky—its 1/day {@b Starburst} (a Force-damage {@spell fireball|XPHB}) is the warband's deadliest single moment. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} for the full encounter.
